events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario: field-service visit, suspension & steering, observed condition: torquing rubber bushings at full droop (TRD - preload error). Work rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance.

We need decide pass/fail. Condition: torquing rubber bushings at full droop means installing/torquing suspension bushings when suspension is at full droop (extended) rather than at ride height or specified position. This induces preload/twist in rubber bushings when vehicle at normal ride height, causing binding, premature wear, alignment issues, handling, noise. It's a defect. Decision fail. Risk? Could be medium or high. Suspension preload error can cause abnormal tire wear, handling instability, component fatigue. Field service installed operating asset. I'd say high? But not critical immediate safety maybe. Let's assess. TRD preload error: if control arm bushings torqued at full droop, at ride height the bushing is twisted, causing permanent torsional preload. This can affect alignment specs (caster/camber/toe changes), cause pulling, uneven tire wear, bushing failure. Risk high due to safety/handling. Component condition installed-defective and non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 70%. Value? Not asked numeric reading; maybe 0 or could indicate preload angle. But value is numeric reading/computed quantity when task asks. Here task asks estimate or interpret measurable condition. Could set value to represent estimated bushing preload twist, e.g., 8 degrees? But no actual measurement. Maybe value 0 if not applicable? Schema says use value for numeric reading or computed quantity when task asks for one. It doesn't ask for numeric reading exactly, but says estimate measurable condition. Could set value to 0 and explain in rationale. Or set value to null? Schema says number 0 default. Use 0.

Confidence high 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, sound_source empty. workable empty. Findings: bushings torqued at full droop causing torsional preload; alignment will be out of spec at ride height; premature bushing wear. Actions: loosen fasteners, support suspension at ride height or OEM specified position, retorque to spec; recheck alignment. Rationale: rubber bushings must be tightened at vehicle ride height or specified loaded position to avoid twisting and preload; torquing at full droop violates OEM procedure and alignment specs. References: OEM service info, alignment specs. Could include generic.

Need output exactly JSON object, no prose. Ensure valid JSON.
